NAME: Device theft
DESCRIPTION: Theft of a computer or physical information system device located in a public area.

NAME: Weak passwords
DESCRIPTION: Passwords that can be easily guessed or inferred by attackers.

NAME: Improper configuration
DESCRIPTION: Incorrectly configured network ports, firewall rules, and other settings.

NAME: Vulnerability
DESCRIPTION: Security weaknesses that exist in software, operating systems, or firmware.

NAME: Internet-facing assets
DESCRIPTION: Assets such as web servers that are connected to the public Internet.

NAME: Shared DB or directory
DESCRIPTION: Databases or directories accessible and used by multiple members of an organization.

NAME: Obsolete products
DESCRIPTION: Devices, databases, or applications no longer supported by the vendor or manufacturer.

NAME: Injection flaws
DESCRIPTION: Security vulnerabilities where unvalidated input allows malicious commands to alter system behavior or steal data.

NAME: Custom APIs
DESCRIPTION: User-defined APIs developed to interact with specific applications or services.

NAME: Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)
DESCRIPTION: Unique identifiers for network groups on the Internet, which attackers can use to discover an organization's IP range and conduct scans or intrusion attempts.

NAME: SSL Certificates and Attribution
DESCRIPTION: Digital certificates used to encrypt communications between a web server and a client.

NAME: WHOIS records, contacts and history
DESCRIPTION: Domain registration information and contacts that can be leveraged for social engineering or attack planning by analyzing network structure.

NAME: TCP/IP ports
DESCRIPTION: Specific endpoints through which data is sent and received on a network.

NAME: Public and private cloud
DESCRIPTION: Misconfigurations in cloud infrastructure that expose sensitive services or data to the public Internet.

NAME: Web server services such as emails and databases
DESCRIPTION: Services such as email or databases provided by a web server.

NAME: Shadow IT
DESCRIPTION: Unauthorized use of software or other IT resources without organizational approval.

NAME: Poor implementation of access controls
DESCRIPTION: Inadequate implementation of access control policies or procedures, leading to increased security risks.

NAME: Malicious insiders
DESCRIPTION: Insiders with hostile intent who leak or destroy information to harm the organization or for personal gain.

NAME: Social engineering
DESCRIPTION: Techniques that exploit human trust or relationships to obtain sensitive information.

NAME: Insufficient physical security controls
DESCRIPTION: Weak physical access controls that allow unauthorized individuals to enter buildings or restricted areas.

NAME: Authentication issues
DESCRIPTION: Problems arising from policies or procedures used to verify user identities in systems or applications.
